item.title: Germans Switch to EVs at Record Pace Thanks to Government Bonus
item.description: More German drivers switched to an electric vehicle in the first quarter of this year than ever before after the government launched a major subsidy program that can shave as much as €6,000 ($6,967) off the price of a new car.

item.title: HSBC, Barclays Among Banks Set to Lose Cambridge University Cash
item.description: Barclays Plc and HSBC Holdings Plc are among UK banks set to lose cash holdings from Cambridge University as it starts pulling hundreds of millions of pounds away from institutions that finance fossil fuels.

item.title: Stonepeak, Lim Family Said to Weigh Withdrawing Yinson Bid
item.description: Yinson Holdings Bhd.’s founding Lim family and Stonepeak Partners are considering withdrawing a plan to take the energy infrastructure company private, according to people familiar with the information.

item.title: London’s Expats Struggle to Adapt to Extreme Heat Without Air Conditioning
item.description: Vanessa Chan, who moved to London from Hong Kong three years ago, has been stunned by the extreme heat she’s experienced in the UK capital over the past few days.

item.title: China’s Crop Belt Faces Flood Risk as Heavy Rains Arrive Early
item.description: Heavy rains are arriving earlier and lasting longer across several parts of China this year, raising risks for agriculture and disaster management.

item.title: These Countries Are Plotting the End of the Fossil Fuel Era
item.description: Tzeporah Berman, chair of the Fossil Fuel Non-Proliferation Treaty Initiative, hopes to bring the most ambitious nations together to end the use of oil, gas and coal.

item.title: The Swiss Alps Reckon With the Threat of Collapsing Glaciers
item.description: A year after the town of Blatten was destroyed, Switzerland faces difficult decisions over the risks and costs of living in the mountains.

item.title: Data Center Firm Backed by Oaktree Plans to Sell Carbon Credits to Hyperscalers
item.description: Pure Data Centres, a UK firm backed by private credit specialist Oaktree Capital Management, is launching a platform to sell carbon-removal credits to large cloud-computing providers.

item.title: El Niño Threat Prompts Peru to Extend Halt on Anchovy Fishing
item.description: Peru has extended a halt on anchovy fishing due to a warming of the Pacific waters off the country’s coast, as the threat of a weather-roiling El Niño looms.

item.title: Oil Barges Forced to Carry Less Fuel as Rhine Water Level Drops
item.description: Falling water levels on one of Europe’s most important rivers are restricting the amount of oil barges can carry, adding more pressure to the region’s fuel supply chains as the Iran war grinds on.

item.title: Iran War Pollution Spread Across Thousands of Miles, Research
item.description: Fires at oil facilities in March produced a plume the size of Italy.

item.title: Ex-Environment Minister to Quit Carney’s Caucus After Climate Reversals
item.description: A former Canadian environment minister will resign his seat in Prime Minister Mark Carney’s caucus after the government watered down its climate policy.

item.title: Why Building the Best Grid Is a Key to the Future
item.description: As EVs and AI spur a rapid rise in electricity demand, innovators are developing solutions that could bring clean, stable power to people around the world.

item.title: Fusion Startup Thea Raises $100 Million to Build Prototype
item.description: US fusion startup Thea Energy Inc. raised $100 million to develop a demonstration project as surging electricity demand spurs interest in the technology that promises to tap the energy of the stars.

item.title: Climate Venture Firm Leaning Into AI With New $150 Million Fund
item.description: Five years after Transition Ventures was founded to back early-stage climate startups, the London-based venture capital firm has raised a new $150 million fund, with a focus on power, artificial intelligence, robotics and critical materials.

item.title: Solid-State Battery Maker ProLogium to Go Public in SPAC Deal
item.description: ProLogium Technology Co. agreed to merge with a blank-check firm in a deal that the company says values it at about $3.8 billion, joining a growing number of next-generation battery makers going public via SPACs.

item.title: Europe Heat Wave Triggers Tropical Nights and Soaring Fan Sales
item.description: Europe’s first major heat wave of the season will scorch capitals from London to Paris and Madrid through the rest of the week, breaking temperature records and pushing up power demand.

item.title: Japan Power Price Extends Gains as War and Heat Strain Supply
item.description: Japan’s spot power price climbed to its highest level in more than a month as forecasts for unusually hot weather boosted demand, while the conflict in Iran tightened fuel supplies.

item.title: Climate Change Fueling Growth of Antibiotic-Resistant Salmonella
item.description: Climate change is linked to a 10% global increase in antibiotic-resistance genes in Salmonella, according to new research that suggests warming temperatures and shifting rainfall patterns could accelerate the spread of hard-to-treat infections.

item.title: UK and France Break May Records as Heat Scorches Europe
item.description: Driven by a heat dome, temperatures have soared.

item.title: Toxic Fumes From Tehran Oil Fires Affected Area Size of Italy
item.description: When Tehran oil infrastructure caught fire in March following Israeli strikes, the blaze produced toxic fumes detectable across an area the size of Italy, according to fresh research.

item.title: NextEra’s Dominion Deal Expands Its Storm Risk
item.description: The utility giant is expanding into some of the US’ costliest weather zones.

item.title: Kenya in Talks With Karpowership for Emergency Power Supply
item.description: Kenya plans to contract Karpowership, the world’s biggest provider of ship-mounted electricity plants, to provide emergency power for the nation’s growing demands.

item.title: Europe Heat Wave Boosts Solar, Sends Power Prices Negative
item.description: The first major heat wave of the season broke temperature records across northwest Europe, triggered water shortages in the UK and has been linked to several deaths in France.

item.title: Clean Energy Trade Ripe for ‘Smart Money’, Singapore Envoy Says
item.description: Renewables are poised to attract fresh, large-scale investment as energy security and supply chain concerns drive an increase in demand and revenue for the sector, according to Singapore’s chief climate diplomat.

item.title: Saving Supply Chains From Climate Shocks Is a Lure for Investors
item.description: Technologies that help manage risks to goods from extreme temperatures and natural disasters are drawing new attention to the adaptation sector.

item.title: India’s Scorching Nights Strain Power Grid and Threaten Health
item.description: India’s dangerously torrid summer days have long been a climate warning. Now the country is increasingly dealing with sweltering nights too, as global warming and poor urban planning put millions at risk.

item.title: BHP Cuts Green Push in Iron Ore Segment, Media Report Says
item.description: BHP Group is pulling back on key decarbonization projects in its Western Australian iron ore operations, slowing a climate strategy the miner had once positioned as central to its long-term growth plans, according to leaked internal documents cited by the Guardian and ABC’s Four Corners.

item.title: A Hunt to Find Dubious Chinese Carbon Credits Snapped Up in Europe
item.description: Overvalued offsets are nothing new. But some emissions-reduction projects don’t appear to exist at all, a Bloomberg investigation found

item.title: Oil Sands Firms Can Afford Carbon Capture, Canadian Energy Minister Says
item.description: Canadian Energy Minister Tim Hodgson said he’s “highly confident” that Alberta oil sands companies can absorb the cost of building carbon capture, despite their protests that Canada’s climate rules hurt their global competitiveness.
